mnemonics: R.A.I.D. Resources: Read All Important Docs
F.I.R.E. Framework: Focused, Integrated, Reliable, Examined
keyPoints: Official textbooks and academic papers provide foundational knowledge and established theories.
Online platforms like Coursera or Cybrary offer updated courses on current threats and defenses.
Practical labs and simulation tools (e.g., Wireshark, VirtualBox) are crucial for hands-on skill development.
commonMistakes: Relying solely on one type of resource (e.g., only videos) instead of a balanced mix of theory and practice.
Using outdated or non-credible sources that may contain incorrect or obsolete security information.
Skipping the practical labs and simulations, which are essential for understanding real-world application.
